_comment: Template for skill eval files. Copy this structure for each generated skill stub.

_format_notes.prompt: Realistic user prompt that should trigger the skill. Write it as a real person would type it.
_format_notes.expected_output: Plain English description of what a successful result looks like.
_format_notes.files: Array of file paths to pre-create in the eval workspace (empty for most skills).
_format_notes.expectations: Array of named assertions. Each must be objectively verifiable.

_assertion_rules: Assertions MUST be objectively verifiable -- no subjective quality judgments
Use descriptive kebab-case names that explain what is being tested
Reference exact output characteristics: file existence, content patterns, structure
Good: 'Output contains a markdown table with at least 3 rows'
Good: 'Generated file has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields'

evals.prompt: REPLACE: A realistic prompt a user would type to trigger this skill
evals.expected_output: REPLACE: Description of what success looks like

expectations.name: REPLACE-descriptive-assertion-name
expectations.assertion: REPLACE: Specific, objectively verifiable assertion about the output

expectations.name: REPLACE-second-assertion
expectations.assertion: REPLACE: Another verifiable assertion testing a different aspect

evals.prompt: REPLACE: A different user prompt testing another aspect of the skill
evals.expected_output: REPLACE: What success looks like for this variation

expectations.name: REPLACE-assertion-for-variation
expectations.assertion: REPLACE: Verifiable assertion for this prompt variation

evals.prompt: REPLACE: An edge case or less obvious trigger for the skill
evals.expected_output: REPLACE: What success looks like for this edge case

expectations.name: REPLACE-edge-case-assertion
expectations.assertion: REPLACE: Verifiable assertion for the edge case
